channel.title: Science & Nature News
channel.description: Latest news from BBC Sky at Night Magazine, BBC Science Focus, and Discover Wildlife

item.title: Sombrero-shaped galaxy stuns in new image – captured by the camera tasked with solving the biggest mystery in the Universe
item.description: The Dark Energy Camera's image of Messier 104, the edge-on galaxy known as the Sombrero Galaxy.

item.title: Next full Moon dates 2025
item.description: Find out when the next full Moon is visible, and full Moon names for 2026.

item.title: Flower Moon, 1 May 2026 – rise times and how to see
item.description: Catch the full Moon in May and you will have seen a 'Flower Moon'.

item.title: That bright star near the Flower Moon tonight? That's Spica, one of the brightest stars of springtime
item.description: Can you see a bright star close to the Moon tonight? That's Spica

item.title: Stargazing | What to see in the night sky this month
item.description: What can you see in the night sky tonight? Find out what's on view in our monthly stargazing guide.

item.title: Debris from Halley's Comet is visible in our skies. How to see the Eta Aquariid meteor shower now
item.description: How to see the Eta Aquariids meteor shower this year.

item.title: Halley's Comet meteor shower 2026. Top tips for beginners observing the Eta Aquariids
item.description: Our top tips for beginners heading out to see an Eta Aquariid meteor

item.title: Double suns, more worlds: Tatooine-style planets with two sunsets are more common than thought, says brand new study
item.description: Planets with two suns are more likely to thrive than planets with only one sun, say astronomers

item.title: Light-powered spaceships could get to our nearest star in 20 years. Fiction? Scientists say it could become fact
item.description: Could light-powered spacecraft be humanity's saviour?

item.title: Total solar eclipse August 2026 – Path map, key locations and timings
item.description: How to see this summer's biggest celestial event from Europe, the UK and North America

item.title: Saturn’s moon Titan may hold the building blocks of alien life in its methane lakes and rivers
item.description: Study suggests that life’s chemical precursors could arise in non-water environments

item.title: The cosmic explosion that wouldn't quit. Strange signal from deep space baffles astronomers
A real-life Tatooine? Astronomers discover trio of planets in rare double-sun system
item.description: Get the key facts on this story with our quick, 1-minute read Star system: astronomers have discovered three Earth-sized planets orbiting a binary star system known as TOI-2267 Close proximity: the system is located 190 lightyears from Earth, which is considered relatively nearby in astronomical terms Binary nature: the two stars in the system are cool and orbit each other at a distance just eight times the distance between Earth and the Sun Record breakers: this discovery represents the coldest and most compact pair of stars known to host planets Transit first: the study marks the first time planets have

item.title: The US coastline is heading for an ocean disaster even faster than we thought, study suggests
item.description: New research suggests the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation could weaken by half this century with wide ranging consequences for weather, food and sea levels across the world

item.title: This terrifying 'kraken' was the true apex predator of the dinosaur age, scientists discover
item.description: Lurking in prehistoric seas, a colossal, intelligent hunter the length of four cars was crushing bones and seizing prey

item.title: Monkeys have found a bizarre natural supplement to counteract human junk food
item.description: The macaques of Gibraltar love stealing human junk food, but the treats play havoc on their digestive systems

item.title: The end of sex? How human reproduction could soon change forever
item.description: New technologies could reimagine baby-making as we know it. But will they actually replace tried-and-tested (and enjoyable) methods?

item.title: We could be on the brink of total internet collapse. And there may only be months to stop it
item.description: The latest batch of AI models are revealing a host of vulnerabilities across the worldwide web. Soon, they could be in the wrong hands

item.title: Two supermassive black holes are now heading for a Universe-shaking collision
item.description: In a galaxy far, far away, two black holes are locked in a battle to the death

item.title: Scientists have just mapped all the nerves of the clitoris for the first time
item.description: Researchers hope a new study will act as the “starting point of clitoris science”

item.title: A mega-building in China is now so large that it's slowing Earth's spin
item.description: It's even changing how long a day is

item.title: We’re losing 338 spoken words every day
item.description: We've been nattering less and less each year since 2005

item.title: 6 mind-blowing space missions now set to launch after Artemis II
item.description: From water hunting on the Moon to the closest planet to the Sun

item.title: Artemis II in pictures: 21 jaw-dropping photos, from launch to splashdown
item.description: Ten days, one Moon, and a view we’ve waited half a century to see again

item.title: What your vitamin D levels could reveal about your dementia risk
item.description: Scientists have found that people with higher vitamin D in midlife had less of a key Alzheimer’s protein in their brains years later

item.title: A massive once-in-500-years chimpanzee civil war has broken out
item.description: The largest-known group of wild chimpanzees has split in two. The aftermath has been brutal

item.title: How some people sleep only four hours a night and still feel great
item.description: A small group of humans barely rest at night – and never suffer for it. Science is about to find out why that matters for all of us

item.title: The unsettling truth about medical cannabis and mental health
item.description: Does it help with mental health, or is there something else at play?

item.title: Your reality really could be a simulation, say experts. Here’s why
item.description: As computers grow ever more powerful, the idea we could be living in a simulation is looking less like science fiction every day

item.title: The US is now paying more than any other country for climate change damage, study suggests
item.description: Despite being the biggest carbon emitter, the US is already paying a disproportionate price for the climate crisis

item.title: The 5 biggest myths about taking statins to lower cholesterol
item.description: Statins save lives – so why are so many people wary of taking them?

item.title: I'm a neuroscientist. Here's all you need to know about money to be truly happy
item.description: We all need enough funds to cover our basic needs, but beyond that the connection between wealth and wellness is less clear

item.title: There's no such thing as a shark, suggest scientists
item.description: Analysis of shark DNA has thrown a wrench into our understanding of these sharp-toothed predators

item.title: We’re closer than ever to bringing back life from cryogenic freezing
item.description: Astronauts in cryosleep is one of science fiction's most enduring fantasies – but a new study suggests the gap between fiction and reality just got a little smaller

item.title: An ancient Maya ‘codex’ keeps predicting future events with eerie accuracy
item.description: The Maya civilisation is known for its art and architecture. But as we decipher their surviving cultural relics, we’re discovering Maya astronomy was even more advanced than we had imagined

item.title: The unsettling reasons bowel cancer might be rising among under-50s
item.description: Deaths from the main types of cancer in people under 50 are falling. But for bowel cancer, they’re increasing

item.title: This (very weird) rule has been controlling fashion for 150 years
item.description: Ever wondered why low-rise jeans are making a comeback?

item.title: 14 powerful images from the Environmental Photography Award 2026
item.description: Britta Jaschinski's photo of a glowing sea turtle wins top prize in this year's Environmental Photography Award.

item.title: A venomous pit viper has been hiding in China's misty mountains
item.description: Scientists describe new species of amber-eyed, grass-green snake from China's Giant Panda National Park.

item.title: Millions of armoured creatures are crawling into this US bay. What happens next is wild
item.description: Every April, horseshoe crabs descend on Delaware Bay to spawn, forming the largest horseshoe crab aggregation on the planet.

item.title: "We have watched and waited for this moment." World-first footage shows orangutan using canopy bridge to cross a road in North Sumatra
item.description: The orangutan can be seen carefully making his way along the rope structure, which was put up two years ago to provide the large primates with safe passage between forest fragments.

item.title: Galápagos deep-sea corals vanished for more than 1,000 years. Scientists think they know why
item.description: 5,000 years ago, deep-water corals disappeared from the waters around the Pacific Ocean islands, study finds.

item.title: A remarkable creature lurks in this flooded sinkhole in Mexico. Scientists went in to find it
item.description: This extraordinary animal supports a secret subterranean ecosystem.

item.title: "It’s all very well squeezing the living breath out of your victims before swallowing them whole, but how do you avoid asphyxiating yourself in the process?"
item.description: Researchers reveals how these heavy-bodied snakes consume prey while inhaling and exhaling.

item.title: Satellite images reveal more than 1,000 unmapped coral reefs in Australia
item.description: The discovery of thousands of previously unchartered coral reefs could reinforce conservation efforts in northern Australian waters, say researchers.

item.title: Camera traps film predators descending on cave filled with 40,000 bats. What happens next is staggering
item.description: Incredible footage from Python Cave in Uganda shows leopards, monkeys and eagles all catching and feasting on virus-riddled fruit bats. Scientists say the behaviour has never been seen before.

item.title: The Colorado River vanished for 5 million years. Scientists may have just figured out where it went
item.description: University of California researchers may have solved the mystery of the US river's disappearance from the geological record.

item.title: This diver was exploring the Great Barrier Reef, when he noticed something strange…
item.description: Some fish can communicate with other species by changing the colour of their bodies – as this incredible footage shows.

item.title: 5.5 million bees found beneath New York State cemetery. It's one of the largest populations ever recorded
item.description: Millions of mining bees are living in the soil of Ithaca's East Lawn Cemetery, new study finds

item.title: Fungi may survive extreme conditions of Mars and space, say scientists
item.description: New study suggests microbes could survive the long trip to Mars

item.title: Huge manta ray spotted with chunk of body missing. Divers think they know what caused it
item.description: Manta rays can sometimes heal from injuries such as shark bites but more serious wounds can be fatal.

item.title: Monkeys may eat flowers to alleviate postpartum depression, study finds
item.description: Researchers observed female howler monkeys eating more flowers after they had given birth.

item.title: Three ginormous bison have been craned into the Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History. Here’s why
item.description: After more than 100 years, bison have returned to the Smithsonian Institute in Washington D.C.

item.title: This US shoreline hosts one of Earth's largest shark gatherings. Aerial images just revealed something worrying
item.description: Scientists in Florida are concerned about the impacts of beach nourishment projects on blacktip sharks and other marine species.

item.title: Massive 15kg echidnas once roamed south-east Australia. Evidence of the bizarre animals was found 120 years ago in a cave
item.description: The re-examination of an Ice Age fossil that has spent the last 120 years in the bowels of a museum has filled a major knowledge gap in Australia’s prehistoric fauna…

item.title: This tentacled giant can grow as long as a cricket bat. Tens of thousands are about to gather in South Australia
item.description: The giant cuttlefish aggregation at Point Lowly in South Australia is one of the planet's greatest wildlife spectacles.

item.title: These crabs are racing up the US coastline – and scientists think they know why
item.description: The mangrove fiddler crab is being found hundreds of kilometres north of its traditional range. Here's what's going on

item.title: Strange creature found inside jellyfish. Scientists say it's the "first time this behaviour is documented"
item.description: Jellyfish are known to consume almost anything – but we didn't know they ate this...

item.title: "Unreal" feeding frenzy caught on camera in Mexico
item.description: During Mexico’s sardine run, pelicans and sea lions go head-to-head gobbling down bait fish.

item.title: A great predator has returned the towering cliffs of Yosemite National Park
item.description: New peregrine nest sites herald a record-breaking year for the world’s fastest bird.

item.title: In a remote Himalayan valley, 3 apex predators live peacefully side by side. Camera traps just revealed how they do it
item.description: Leopards, snow leopards and wolves have found a way to live in relative peace, according to a new study.
